# Skill Improvement Proposal: /horde-review

**Agent:** jochi
**Generated by:** kurultai-reflect
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-23T00:19:00Z
**Evidence window:** last 2h, 8 invocations

## Measured Problem
- Phase completion ratio: 0.0 (threshold: 0.60) — all 8 invocations returned success=False
- Effectiveness normalized: 0.0 (threshold: 0.50)
- Retry magnet: YES — triggered /horde-implement escalation after 8th failure in same session
- Distinct sessions spawned: 8 (each failure spawned a new session, no reuse)
- Task being reviewed: "normal-1774002689-188154db — stalled agent triage (zombie hot-potato task, 30 bounces)"

## Observed Failure Pattern

/horde-review was invoked 8 times between 23:18–00:16 on the same or related triage task. Every invocation failed (success=False). The 8 failures spawned 8 distinct session IDs, indicating the agent was retrying from scratch each time rather than diagnosing the prior failure. No diagnostic events (file reads, bash checks) were observed between retry attempts.

On the 9th attempt (00:19), jochi switched to /horde-implement (also failed). This cross-skill escalation without root cause analysis is consistent with a skill that accepts the task but cannot complete it — a "silent failure" pattern rather than an explicit rejection.

**Critical confound:** Fleet-wide model mismatch detected. jochi is running on glm-5 (session.model) while configured for claude-sonnet-4-6 (config_model.resolved). Architecture §3 specifies claude-opus-4-6. This mismatch affects all 5 specialist agents and may be the root cause of skill failures across the fleet — /horde-review may be structurally sound but failing due to the wrong model executing it.

## Proposed Change to SKILL.md

### Option A — Add model validation gate (preferred if model is root cause)

Before Phase 1 of /horde-review, add a preflight check:

```diff
## Phase 0 — Preflight
+ Before beginning review, verify model capability:
+   - If session model is not claude-opus-4-6 or claude-sonnet-4-6:
+     emit MODEL_MISMATCH warning and halt with message:
+     "SKILL_HALT: /horde-review requires opus/sonnet. Session model: {model}. Report to kublai."
```

### Option B — Scope reduction for triage tasks

/horde-review may be over-scoped for "stalled agent triage" work. This is a diagnostics/escalation domain task, not a code review task.

```diff
## When to use this skill
- Code review, implementation quality checks, architectural review
+ NOT appropriate for: agent triage, system health diagnostics, incident investigation
+ For those: use /horde-debug or /systematic-debugging instead
```

## Proposed Skill File Change (diff-style)

```diff
## Phase 0 — Preflight Check (add before current Phase 1)
+ ### Model Gate
+ Verify session model before proceeding:
+   Allowed: claude-opus-4-6, claude-sonnet-4-6
+   If glm-5 or any other model: HALT immediately, emit SKILL_HALT to ledger, notify kublai
+
+ ### Task Fit Gate
+ This skill is appropriate for: code review, implementation quality, architecture review
+ If task description contains "triage", "stalled", "zombie", "hot-potato", "watchdog" keywords:
+   emit TASK_FIT_WARNING and recommend /horde-debug instead
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- /horde-review invocation success rate reaches > 0.70 within 5 invocations after change
- No instance of 3+ consecutive /horde-review failures without a SKILL_HALT event

## Root Cause Priority

**Investigate fleet model mismatch FIRST** (kublai/ogedei action required):
- 5/6 agents running glm-5 vs. configured model
- This may explain /horde-review failures without any change to the skill itself
- If model fix restores success rate, skill change is not needed
